key_themes: Strong underlying organic net inflows and AUM growth
Results expected to beat market expectations
Consistent margin expansion and operational scaling
Resilience despite one-off contract impacts and market volatility
summary: Tatton Asset Management (TAM.L) released a strong trading update in mid-April 2026, reporting underlying organic net inflows of £2.8 billion (15.6% of opening AUM) and underlying AUM up 26.8% to £22.8 billion, driving total AUM/I to £24.2 billion. Despite a one-off contract termination, the company guided for full-year results toward the upper end of market expectations, highlighted continued adviser recruitment and record mortgage activity, and reaffirmed confidence in its growth roadmap. Investor and analyst sentiment is solidly positive, supported by historical outperformance, forecasted double-digit earnings growth, and a track record of high margins.[[1]](https://www.londonstockexchange.com/news-article/TAM/trading-update-and-notice-of-final-results/17546929)[[2]](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/tatton-inflows-beat-estimates-flags-fy26-results-at-upper-end-4614367)
sources: 8+ recent news articles (LSE RNS, Yahoo Finance, Investing.com, Morningstar), ~5-7 relevant X posts on TAM.L trading update (plus broader TAM/Total Addressable Market investor discussions)
